//: Class descriptors for libdevices.so. Scoped to the classes that
matter for the U4025QW path: HID_INTF / IIC_INTF (base transports)
and RTS5409S_HID / RTS5409S_IIC (the actual primary chip). The other
23 chip variants in this binary (PIC16F1454_*, USB57**_*, TUSB***_*,
VL822_*, PS5512_*, MCHP_USB58XX_USB72XX, etc.) are dead code for the
U4025QW — not typing them keeps the JSON tractable.
IIC_INTF mirrors the libdisplay.so.json / libpdc.so.json descriptors.
HID_INTF is a sibling — same abstract-transport pattern but USB-HID
rather than i2c. ModuleMessage is opaque (we just need pointer typing).

0x130.name: ddcci_csum
0x130.//: (IIC_INTF*, u8 slave=0x6E, u8 prefix=0x51, u8* buf, u8 len) -> u8.
Returns the XOR-checksum byte appended after the DDC/CI payload
the algorithm XORs all bytes including the dest-addr 0x6E and prefix
0x51, with a 0x3F seed for the initial bias).

classes.name: HID_INTF
classes.//: Abstract HID-transport base. Sibling of IIC_INTF — same chip families
implement both interfaces, switching between USB-HID and i2c tunneling.
For RTS5409S the HID path is what the U4025QW actually exercises (the
primary appears as a HID device, all i2c-tunnel commands tunnel through
USB-HID feature reports via the 0xC6 / 0xD6 opcodes).
Vtable slots inferred from call sites in libdevices and the HID_INTF
method declarations at libdevices.c:52283-52659.

0x20.name: open_pid
0x20.//: (HID_INTF*, u16 vid, u16 pid, int idx, u8 speed)

0x48.name: write_hid
0x48.//: (HID_INTF*, u8 slave, u8* buf, u8 count) -> u32 — outbound i2c-tunnel via HID feature report

0x50.name: read_hid
0x50.//: (HID_INTF*, u8 slave, u64 reg_packed, u8 count, u8* out, u8 len) -> u32 — inbound i2c-tunnel

0xc8.name: open_int
0xc8.//: open(int idx, u8 speed) variant — chooses by device list index

classes.name: RTS5409S_HID
classes.//: RealTek RTS5409S USB hub MCU, accessed via USB-HID. Constructor
at libdevices.c:66317 zero-inits 0x40, vftable, 16-byte blocks at
0x10/0x20/0x48, sets this+8=0x11000bda (VID=0x0BDA RealTek,
PID=0x1100), sets this+0x30=1, then zeros 0x60..0x118 (0xc1 bytes,
the HID-output report buffer) and 0x121..0x1da (the HID-input
report buffer), and finally zeros 0x1da..0x21a (chip state) and
sets size 0x224.
The wire-format is visible in RTS5409S_HID::write at 66365:
buf[0..1] = 0x4000 (report id + report-len high)
buf[2]    = 0xC6 (vendor write opcode) [or 0xD6 for read in read()]
buf[7]    = count
buf[9]    = i2c slave (param_1)
buf[11]   = i2c speed (= this+0x40)
buf[0x41..] = payload
total HID report = 0xC1 bytes

fields.name: vid_pid
fields.//: Packed VID/PID. Constructor sets 0x11000bda = pid 0x1100, vid 0x0bda.

fields.name: open_flag
fields.//: Set to 1 in ctor — selected by open() to pick which path to use.

fields.name: i2c_speed
fields.//: Bus speed byte copied to buf[11] of every write/read. 0=standard,
1..7=faster variants. Set by set_bus_speed().

fields.name: last_error
fields.//: Last error code returned. 0x81=bad params, 0x20=write fail, 0x21=read fail.

fields.name: hid_dev
fields.//: hidapi device handle (opaque).

fields.name: out_report_byte1
fields.//: 0x58/0x59 together hold the report-id pair 0x4000 written little-endian.

fields.name: out_opcode
fields.//: Vendor opcode: 0xC6 for i2c write, 0xD6 for i2c read.

fields.name: out_count
fields.//: Payload byte count. For i2c writes this is the data length copied to +0x99.

fields.name: out_i2c_target
fields.//: i2c 8-bit slave address (already left-shifted).

fields.name: out_i2c_speed
fields.//: Copied from this->i2c_speed at the start of every write/read.

fields.name: out_payload
fields.//: Start of the variable-length payload area within the 0xC1-byte output report.

fields.name: out_padding_0x111
fields.//: Tail-byte of the output report; zeroed before every send to pad the report to 0xC1.

fields.name: in_report_byte0
fields.//: First byte of the 0xC1-byte HID input report buffer starting at +0x119.

fields.name: in_payload
fields.//: Start of the input-report payload (the chip's reply bytes). The kernel strips
the report-id prefix so byte 0 of the payload lives at +0x11a.

fields.name: in_byte0x1d2
fields.//: Tail-byte of the input report area, zeroed before every read.

fields.name: state_0x1da
fields.//: Per-chip state continuation block (16 bytes here + 16 + 16 + 16 = up to 0x21a).

classes.name: RTS5409S_IIC
classes.//: i2c-interface wrapper around RTS5409S_HID. Constructor at
libdevices.c:68367 embeds an RTS5409S_HID at +0x20 (calling
RTS5409S_HID::RTS5409S_HID((RTS5409S_HID*)(this+0x20))) and
stores a self-pointer to the embedded HID at +0x10. Same chip,
different interface — i2c calls route through the embedded HID's
USB-HID tunnel.

fields.name: hid
fields.//: Pointer to the embedded RTS5409S_HID at +0x20. Set by ctor.
Most IIC methods delegate to (*this->hid).<method>(...).

fields.name: hid_embedded
fields.//: Embedded RTS5409S_HID instance — IIC IS-A HID.

classes.name: HIDI2C_MIDDLE_LAYER
classes.//: Thin shim providing ISP_WriteI2C / ISP_ReadI2C / ISP_FetchFWData.
Body delegates to a *global* USB7206_HID pointer named `usb_ptr`
(not a this-field), so no class-instance fields to type — the
class is effectively stateless. Methods exist at libdevices.c:61525.
Not on the U4025QW path (USB7206 isn't this monitor's chip);
kept here for completeness with the original analysis claim.
